instruction: Set python and pip defaults for this user so that `python` runs Python 3 and `pip` points to pip3, without removing system binaries. Then create ~/Desktop/python_default_report.txt with two lines: python=python3 and pip=pip3.
source: 外部参考来源：Canonical tutorial - Command line for beginners (https://ubuntu.com/tutorials/command-line-for-beginners)

result.command: pyv=$(python --version 2>&1); python_ok='false'; echo "$pyv" | grep -q '^Python 3\.' && python_ok='true'; pipv=$(pip --version 2>&1); pip_ok='false'; echo "$pipv" | grep -q 'python 3' && pip_ok='true'; expected=$(printf 'python=python3\npip=pip3\n'); actual=$(cat ~/Desktop/python_default_report.txt 2>/dev/null); file_ok='false'; [ "$actual" = "$expected" ] && file_ok='true'; echo "python_ok=$python_ok"; echo "pip_ok=$pip_ok"; echo "file_ok=$file_ok"
